title: carex sempervirens subsp. granitica
intro: Carex sempervirens subsp. granitica, also known as the granite-leaved sedge, is a subspecies of the evergreen sedge plant (Carex sempervirens) that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Carex sempervirens subsp. granitica include its ability to reduce inflammation, treat wounds, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it has also been used in traditional herbal remedies for centuries due to its various health benefits.
